properties.value: INFTs: Tokenizing AI Agents with ERC-7857

concepts.title: INFTs: Tokenizing AI Agents with ERC-7857
The AI Agent Storage Pattern
Indexing 0G Data with Goldsky
concepts.content:
Key ideas:
- Subgraphs: GraphQL API over 0G contract events
- Mirror: real-time streaming of events to your database
- Point Goldsky to 0G RPC endpoints and your contract ABIs

0G Labs recommends Goldsky as the indexing solution for 0G contract data. Raw on-chain event data is difficult to query efficiently — Goldsky transforms it into queryable APIs and database streams.

**Goldsky Subgraphs:**
Deploy a GraphQL API over your contract's event history. Query patterns like 'all files uploaded in the last 24 hours' or 'total storage used by address 0x...'.

```graphql
# Query uploaded files
{
  fileUploads(first: 10, orderBy: timestamp, orderDirection: desc) {
    id
    uploader
    rootHash
    size
    timestamp
  }
}
```

**Goldsky Mirror:**
Streams contract events directly to your database in real-time. Perfect for dashboards that need sub-second latency.

```yaml
# goldsky mirror config
sources:
  - type: ethereum
    name: 0g-storage-events
    networks:
      - name: 0g-testnet
        rpc_url: https://evmrpc-testnet.0g.ai
        start_block: 0
    contracts:
      - address: '0x22E03a6A89B950F1c82ec5e74F8eCa321a105296' # Flow contract
        abi: ./flow-abi.json
        events: [FileUploaded, MerkleTreeBuilt]
sinks:
  - type: postgres
    name: my-dashboard-db
    connection: $DATABASE_URL
```

**Use Goldsky when you need:**
- Dashboard showing real-time storage usage
- Analytics over compute job history
- Notification system when specific contracts emit events
- Historical data analysis over 0G chain events

For simple explorers, you can also use the existing block explorers: `https://chainscan-galileo.0g.ai` (testnet) or `https://storageScan.0g.ai` (storage-specific).

concepts.title: Capstone: Building a Full-Stack 0G Application

quiz.question: In a full-stack 0G app, how do smart contracts reference files stored on 0G Storage?
options: Using the rootHash (Merkle tree root) as a content-addressed pointer
quiz.correctAnswer: Using the rootHash (Merkle tree root) as a content-addressed pointer
